title: podman run fails inside of a Docker container in AWS Batch Job
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
I launch an AWS batch job - a docker container, with podman inside. Podman successfully pulls a container, and dies on running  it with `invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference`. The issue appeared after podman v3.2.1 published.
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.Create a Docker image, based on alpine with such endpoint: podman pulls a docker image, and runs it

2.Create AWS batch job, associated with the docker image from item 1

3.Launch the batch job, get `invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference` at job's logs

**Describe the results you received:**
A stack trace I collected from logs of the cloud job:
```
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference,
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x18 pc=0x560d50411b28],
github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/cgroups.getAvailableControllers(0xc000376870, 0xc0002a9000, 0x0, 0x15, 0xc0005f1038, 0x15, 0x1),
goroutine 1 [running]:,
pkg/cgroups/cgroups.go:377 +0xfa,
libpod/oci_conmon_linux.go:1401 +0x26e,
pkg/cgroups/cgroups.go:171 +0x688,
github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod.(*ConmonOCIRuntime).moveConmonToCgroupAndSignal(0xc0005c2dc0, 0xc0005c2280, 0xc00054c6e0, 0xc00051f240, 0x0, 0x0),
github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/cgroups.New(0xc0005f1038, 0x15, 0xc000254df0, 0xc0005f1038, 0x15, 0xc000000180),
github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod.(*ConmonOCIRuntime).createOCIContainer(0xc0005c2dc0, 0xc0005c2280, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0),
github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod.(*Container).init(0xc0005c2280, 0x560d51639988, 0xc0001ac030, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0),
github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod.(*Container).prepareToStart(0xc0005c2280, 0x560d51639988, 0xc0001ac030, 0x7f76f3b50501, 0x0, 0x0),
libpod/oci_conmon_linux.go:1157 +0x1305,
libpod/oci_conmon_linux.go:252 +0xb7,
github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod.(*ConmonOCIRuntime).CreateContainer(0xc0005c2dc0, 0xc0005c2280, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0),
libpod/container_internal.go:1070 +0x1ae,
libpod/container_api.go:114 +0x9f,
github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/domain/infra/abi/terminal.StartAttachCtr(0x560d51639988, 0xc0001ac030, 0xc0005c2280, 0xc000134008, 0xc000134010, 0xc000134000, 0x560d50fac9e7, 0xd, 0x101, 0x0, ...),
pkg/domain/infra/abi/terminal/terminal_linux.go:91 +0x3d3,
libpod/container_internal.go:830 +0x315,
github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod.(*Container).StartAndAttach(0xc0005c2280, 0x560d51639988, 0xc0001ac030, 0xc0005da930, 0x560d50fac9e7, 0xd, 0xc00004e420, 0xc0004da601, 0x0, 0x0, ...),
github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/domain/infra/abi.(*ContainerEngine).ContainerRun(0xc0000104f8, 0x560d51639988, 0xc0001ac030, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x560d50fac9e7, 0xd, 0xc000134010, 0xc000134000, ...),
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteC(0x560d52010320, 0xc000132020, 0x560d513be7e0, 0x560d520d07c8),
vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:960 +0x375,
cmd/podman/containers/run.go:188 +0x5b5,
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).execute(0x560d51ffaa20, 0xc000138020, 0x20, 0x22, 0x560d51ffaa20, 0xc000138020),
vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:852 +0x472,
pkg/domain/infra/abi/containers.go:898 +0x37f,
github.com/containers/podman/v3/cmd/podman/containers.run(0x560d51ffaa20, 0xc000520800, 0x9, 0x20, 0x0, 0x0),
vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:890,
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).Execute(...),
main.Execute(),
vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:897,
main.main(),
cmd/podman/root.go:90 +0xe9,
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteContext(...),
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
podman v.3.2.1 runs containers successfully inside docker container as an AWS BatchJob

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
v.3.1.2 and previous versions worked well for all environments I need.. All podman versions in that configuration (podman inside docker) runs perfectly on my local machine.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.1,
API Version:  3.2.1,
Go Version:   go1.16.5,
Git Commit:   d183f0610827ab235b01ae4c6f8c8f2858d50284,
Built:        Mon Jun 14 17:24:45 2021,
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64,
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
Then the trace stack I provided above
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
apk list podman
podman-3.2.1-r0 x86_64 {podman} (Apache-2.0) [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
AWS Batch Job, Docker container from Amazon ECR, based on Alpine Linux 3.13

comments.body:
could it be fixed with fc9868e2247d2eb651586bd4b3e9a4ece3562fa0 ?

https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10668
yes, I think that is the correct fix.  Closing the issue
